questions.question: A student uses a measuring cylinder to find the volume of ten identical steel balls. The water level rises from $20\text{ cm}^3$ to $50\text{ cm}^3$. If one ball has a mass of $27\text{ g}$, what is the density of the steel?
questions.explanation:
Volume of 10 balls $= 30\text{ cm}^3$, so volume of 1 ball $= 3\text{ cm}^3$. Density $= 27\text{ g} / 3\text{ cm}^3 = 9.0\text{ g/cm}^3$. 

**Common mistake:** Dividing the total mass of one ball by the total volume of ten balls ($0.9$).

questions.question: The table shows the densities of four different liquids. Which liquid will float on top of all the others if they are immiscible?
questions.explanation:
The liquid with the lowest density will always float on top of denser liquids. 

**Common mistake:** Choosing the liquid with the highest numerical value, confusing 'top layer' with 'most substance'.

questions.question: A graph is plotted with Mass on the y-axis and Volume on the x-axis for a particular substance. What does the gradient of the straight line represent?
questions.explanation:
Gradient $= \text{change in } y / \text{change in } x = \text{Mass} / \text{Volume}$, which is the definition of density. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the gradient is weight, which would require a Mass vs Weight graph.

questions.question: Which set of equipment is required to determine the density of an irregularly shaped piece of metal that sinks in water?
questions.explanation:
An electronic balance measures mass and a measuring cylinder (using displacement) measures the volume of irregular objects. 

**Common mistake:** Selecting a metre rule, which can only measure regular shapes easily.

questions.question: A block of wood has a density of $600\text{ kg/m}^3$. A second block of the same wood has double the mass. What is the density of the second block?
questions.explanation:
Density is a constant property for a uniform material regardless of mass or size. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking that doubling the mass also doubles the density.
